title: building components
text: simplest component function wraps tag fixed structure, exposing parts change arguments. really good example button: app depending styling, snippet might look like : want re-use snippet parts app, need convert component: ’d default button rendered. sometimes, need tweak button little bit. let’s identify moving parts: “submit”: button submits form data server. “reset”: button resets controls initial values. “button”: button default behaviour, nothing pressed default. class: ’d need pass [custom] classes button. extra classes dictate ’s styling behaviour clicked. label: button app probably different label. , let’s now improve Button() component: see works? even added function docs . practically, ’d call component like : look:
code: btn <- tags$button(   type = "button",   class = "btn btn-primary",   "Click Me!" ) cat(   render(btn) ) Button <- function() {   tags$button(     type = "button",     class = "btn btn-primary",     "Click Me!"   ) }  cat(   render(Button()) ) #' A Button #' #' @param label String /// Required. #'              The button label. #' #' @param type String /// Optional. #'             Button type. Either: #'             - "button" (default) #'             - "submit" #'             - "reset" #' #' @param class Character vector /// Optional. #'              CSS classes to apply to the button. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Button <- function(   label,   type = c("button", "submit", "reset"),   class = NULL ) {   type <- match.arg(arg = type)    tags$button(     type = type,     class = c("btn", class),     label   ) } btn_a <- Button(   label = "Are you sure?",   class = "btn-warning" )  btn_b <- Button(   label = "Submit",   type = "submit",   class = "btn-primary" )  btn_c <- Button(   label = "Confirm",   class = "btn-success" )  btn_d <- Button(   label = "Delete",   class = "btn-danger" ) my_btns <- tag_list(   btn_a,   btn_b,   btn_c,   btn_d )  cat(   render(my_btns) )

title: building components
text: say want Badge() component. let’s first define color variants: variants map directly bootstrap utility classes. comes Badge(): caller controls label colour; structure fixed inside function. new deprecated stable components can wrap components. Alert() component can embed Badge() severity label, keeping independently reusable. , depending styling classes apply, might look like app: Alert() example, dismiss_btn conditional child, ’s set NULL default. hypertext tags drop NULL children properties.
code: #' Bootstrap Color Variants #' #' @return Character vector. color_variants <- function() {   c(     "primary",     "secondary",     "success",     "danger",     "warning",     "info",     "light",     "dark"   ) } #' A Badge #' #' @param label String /// Required. #'              Label to show on the badge. #' #' @param variant String /// Optional. #'                Color variant of the badge. #'                Must be one of `color_variants()`. #'                Defaults to the "primary" variant. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Badge <- function(   label,   variant = color_variants()  ) {   variant <- match.arg(arg = variant)    tags$span(     class = c(       "badge",       paste0("text-bg-", variant)     ),     label   ) }  badge_a <- Badge("new") badge_b <- Badge("deprecated", variant = "danger") badge_c <- Badge("stable", variant = "success") cat(   render(badge_a),   render(badge_b),   render(badge_c) ) #' An Alert #' #' @param message String /// Required. #'                Message to show on the alert. #' #' @param variant String /// Optional. #'                Color variant of the alert. #'                Must be one of `color_variants()`. #'                Defaults to the "primary" variant. #' #' @param dismissible String /// Optional. #'                 Should the alert be dismissible? Either: #'                 - "no" (default) #'                 - "yes" #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Alert <- function(   message,   variant = color_variants(),   dismissible = c("no", "yes") ) {   variant <- match.arg(arg = variant)   dismissible <- match.arg(arg = dismissible)    severity_label <- Badge(     label = variant,     variant = variant   )    classes <- c(     "alert",     paste0("alert-", variant)   )   dismiss_btn <- NULL   if (dismissible == "yes") {     classes <- c(       classes,       "alert-dismissible fade show"     )      dismiss_btn <- tags$button(       type = "button",       class = "btn-close",       `data-bs-dismiss` = "alert",       `aria-label` = "Close"     )   }    tags$div(     role = "alert",     class = classes,     severity_label,     " ",     message,     dismiss_btn   ) }  alert_a <- Alert(   message = "Your session will expire in 5 minutes.",   variant = "warning" )  alert_b <- Alert(   message = "An error while processing your request.",   variant = "danger" )  alert_c <- Alert(   message = "This is some info you need to know.",   variant = "info",   dismissible = "yes" )  alert_d <- Alert(   message = "Report rendered successfully.",   variant = "success",   dismissible = "yes" ) cat(   render(alert_a),   render(alert_b),   render(alert_c),   render(alert_d) )

title: building components
text: sometimes, need pass unknown number tags children tag. ’s dots (...) come . dots useful component owns outer structure, caller owns inner content. let’s make Card() component. header structure fixed, body can contain number children. ’s might look: report rendered successfully. Current release channel: stable inside Card(), ... forwarded tags$div(class = "card-body"). means call site can decide whether body plain text, paragraph, badge, button, mix .
code: #' A Card #' #' @param title String /// Required. #'              Title to show in the card header. #' #' @param ... Tags, text, or other renderable objects /// Optional. #'            Body content for the card. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Card <- function(   title,   ... ) {   tags$div(     class = "card my-2",     tags$div(       class = "card-header",       tags$strong(title)     ),     tags$div(       class = "card-body",       ...     )   ) }  card_a <- Card(   title = "Report status",   tags$p(     class = "card-text",     "The report rendered successfully."   ) )  card_b <- Card(   title = "Package status",   tags$p(     class = "card-text",     "Current release channel: ",     Badge("stable", variant = "success")   ),   Button(     label = "View details",     class = "btn-sm btn-primary"   ) ) cards <- tag_list(   card_a,   card_b )  cat(   render(cards) )

title: building components
text: can map items return hypertext.tag objects. mapping, use list-returning R functions eg. lapply(), Map(), etc. plain lists returned functions flattened automatically passed children tag. ’s ListGroup() component maps character vector <li> children: Write component Render Reuse lapply() returns plain list tags$li() nodes. list passed inside tags$ul(), hypertext treats item child <ul>. use tag_list() want group sibling nodes without adding parent element. use plain mapped list sibling nodes already parent tag, like <ul> .
code: #' A List Group #' #' @param items Character vector /// Required. #'              Items to show in the list group. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export ListGroup <- function(items) {   tags$ul(     class = "list-group list-group-numbered my-2",     lapply(       X = items,       FUN = function(item) {         tags$li(           class = "list-group-item",           item         )       }     )   ) }  todos <- c(   "Write the component",   "Render it",   "Reuse it" )  todo_list <- ListGroup(todos) cat(   render(todo_list) )

title: building components
text: components functions return hypertext.tag objects. can bake styling (css) behaviour (js) , ’re composable.

title: svg with hypertext
text: svg needs canvas. viewBox says coordinate system use. inside canvas, every shape just another tag. named arguments become svg attributes. cx, cy, r, fill, stroke rendered directly onto <circle> element.
code: orb <- tags$svg(   xmlns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",   viewBox = "0 0 120 120",   width = "120",   height = "120",   tags$circle(     cx = "60",     cy = "60",     r = "42",     fill = "#22d3ee",     stroke = "#0f172a",     `stroke-width` = "4"   ) ) cat(   render(orb) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: one shape, can place several shapes inside svg. coordinate system starts top-left. x increases move right; y increases move . can already see pattern: svg special hypertext. tag nesting model, different set elements attributes.
code: shape_tray <- tags$svg(   xmlns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",   viewBox = "0 0 520 150",   width = "520",   height = "150",   tags$rect(     x = "0",     y = "0",     width = "520",     height = "150",     rx = "22",     fill = "#020617"   ),   tags$circle(     cx = "75",     cy = "75",     r = "38",     fill = "#22d3ee"   ),   tags$rect(     x = "150",     y = "37",     width = "76",     height = "76",     rx = "18",     fill = "#a78bfa"   ),   tags$line(     x1 = "282",     y1 = "110",     x2 = "358",     y2 = "40",     stroke = "#facc15",     `stroke-width` = "12",     `stroke-linecap` = "round"   ),   tags$path(     d = "M410 102 C430 20, 478 20, 498 102",     fill = "none",     stroke = "#fb7185",     `stroke-width` = "10",     `stroke-linecap` = "round"   ) ) cat(   render(shape_tray) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: going write svg, let’s remove repeated wrapper. small Svg() component can namespace, dimensions, viewBox; callers can pass children .... now canvas reusable: ... technique html components. component owns outer structure; caller owns inner content.
code: #' An SVG Canvas #' #' @param width Number /// Required. #'              Width of the rendered svg. #' #' @param height Number /// Required. #'               Height of the rendered svg. #' #' @param ... Tags, text, or other renderable objects /// Optional. #'            Children to place inside the svg. #' #' @param viewBox String /// Optional. #'                SVG viewBox. Defaults to `0 0 width height`. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Svg <- function(   width,   height,   ...,   viewBox = paste("0 0", width, height) ) {   tags$svg(     xmlns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",     viewBox = viewBox,     width = width,     height = height,     role = "img",     ...   ) } night_sky <- Svg(   width = 420,   height = 180,   tags$rect(     x = "0",     y = "0",     width = "420",     height = "180",     rx = "24",     fill = "#111827"   ),   tags$circle(cx = "90", cy = "70", r = "34", fill = "#38bdf8"),   tags$circle(cx = "205", cy = "98", r = "50", fill = "#8b5cf6"),   tags$circle(cx = "322", cy = "62", r = "26", fill = "#fb7185") ) cat(   render(night_sky) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: can go one level deeper make components shapes . now scene just composition:
code: #' An Orb #' #' @param cx Number /// Required. #'           Horizontal centre. #' #' @param cy Number /// Required. #'           Vertical centre. #' #' @param r Number /// Required. #'          Radius. #' #' @param fill String /// Optional. #'             Fill colour. #' #' @param opacity Number /// Optional. #'                Shape opacity. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Orb <- function(   cx,   cy,   r,   fill = "currentColor",   opacity = 1 ) {   tags$circle(     cx = cx,     cy = cy,     r = r,     fill = fill,     opacity = opacity   ) }  #' A Spark #' #' @param x Number /// Required. #'          Horizontal centre. #' #' @param y Number /// Required. #'          Vertical centre. #' #' @param size Number /// Optional. #'             Spark size. #' #' @param color String /// Optional. #'              Stroke colour. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export Spark <- function(   x,   y,   size = 10,   color = "#fde68a" ) {   tags$g(     stroke = color,     `stroke-width` = "3",     `stroke-linecap` = "round",     tags$line(x1 = x - size, y1 = y, x2 = x + size, y2 = y),     tags$line(x1 = x, y1 = y - size, x2 = x, y2 = y + size)   ) } scene <- Svg(   width = 520,   height = 220,   tags$rect(     x = "0",     y = "0",     width = "520",     height = "220",     rx = "28",     fill = "#020617"   ),   Orb(cx = 115, cy = 112, r = 66, fill = "#22d3ee", opacity = 0.95),   Orb(cx = 230, cy = 94, r = 88, fill = "#8b5cf6", opacity = 0.72),   Orb(cx = 348, cy = 130, r = 58, fill = "#fb7185", opacity = 0.86),   Spark(x = 415, y = 56, size = 13),   Spark(x = 78, y = 46, size = 9, color = "#bae6fd"),   Spark(x = 460, y = 150, size = 8, color = "#fecdd3") )  cat(   render(scene) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: svg elements define things instead drawing things immediately. <defs> definitions live. gradients common example: define gradient , give id, reference url(#id). let’s make gradient component. inside small mark: gradient drawn . becomes useful another element references . idea defining function calling later.
code: #' A Linear Gradient #' #' @param id String /// Required. #'           Gradient id. #' #' @param colors Character vector /// Required. #'               Colours in the gradient. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export LinearGradient <- function(id, colors) {   stops <- lapply(     X = seq_along(colors),     FUN = function(i) {       offset <- if (length(colors) == 1L) {         0       } else {         (i - 1) / (length(colors) - 1) * 100       }        tags$stop(         offset = paste0(round(offset), "%"),         `stop-color` = colors[[i]]       )     }   )    tags$linearGradient(     id = id,     x1 = "0%",     y1 = "0%",     x2 = "100%",     y2 = "100%",     stops   ) } signal_mark <- Svg(   width = 520,   height = 220,   tags$defs(     LinearGradient(       id = "aurora",       colors = c("#22d3ee", "#8b5cf6", "#fb7185")     )   ),   tags$rect(     x = "0",     y = "0",     width = "520",     height = "220",     rx = "28",     fill = "#020617"   ),   tags$path(     d = "M42 145 C112 35, 188 205, 258 96 S395 28, 478 132",     fill = "none",     stroke = "url(#aurora)",     `stroke-width` = "18",     `stroke-linecap` = "round"   ),   tags$text(     x = "42",     y = "58",     fill = "#f8fafc",     `font-size` = "24",     `font-family` = "inherit",     `font-weight` = "700",     "inline svg, built in r"   ),   tags$text(     x = "42",     y = "86",     fill = "#94a3b8",     `font-size` = "14",     `font-family` = "inherit",     "components, gradients, paths, and text"   ) )  cat(   render(signal_mark) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: svg coordinates just numbers, data graphics normal R programming. compute x, y, width, height, pass values attributes. coordinate trick remember svg starts top-left. bar taller, height gets bigger y value gets smaller. lapply() returns plain list <g> nodes. list passed inside Svg(), hypertext flattens sibling children svg.
code: #' A Bar Chart #' #' @param values Named numeric vector /// Required. #'               Values to plot. #' #' @param width Number /// Optional. #'              SVG width. #' #' @param height Number /// Optional. #'               SVG height. #' #' @param title String /// Optional. #'              Chart title. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export BarChart <- function(   values,   width = 560,   height = 260,   title = "signal strength" ) {   labels <- names(values)   if (is.null(labels)) {     labels <- as.character(seq_along(values))   }    pad_left <- 46   pad_right <- 28   pad_top <- 62   pad_bottom <- 46   gap <- 12    plot_w <- width - pad_left - pad_right   plot_h <- height - pad_top - pad_bottom   bar_w <- (plot_w - gap * (length(values) - 1)) / length(values)   max_value <- max(values)   if (max_value == 0) {     max_value <- 1   }    bars <- lapply(     X = seq_along(values),     FUN = function(i) {       value <- values[[i]]       bar_h <- value / max_value * plot_h       bar_x <- pad_left + (i - 1) * (bar_w + gap)       bar_y <- pad_top + plot_h - bar_h       label_x <- bar_x + bar_w / 2        tags$g(         tags$rect(           x = round(bar_x, 1),           y = round(bar_y, 1),           width = round(bar_w, 1),           height = round(bar_h, 1),           rx = "10",           fill = "url(#bars)",           opacity = "0.94"         ),         tags$text(           x = round(label_x, 1),           y = round(bar_y - 10, 1),           `text-anchor` = "middle",           `font-size` = "12",           `font-family` = "inherit",           `font-weight` = "700",           fill = "#f8fafc",           round(value, 1)         ),         tags$text(           x = round(label_x, 1),           y = height - 18,           `text-anchor` = "middle",           `font-size` = "12",           `font-family` = "inherit",           fill = "#cbd5e1",           labels[[i]]         )       )     }   )    Svg(     width = width,     height = height,     tags$defs(       LinearGradient(         id = "bars",         colors = c("#22d3ee", "#a78bfa")       )     ),     tags$rect(       x = "0",       y = "0",       width = width,       height = height,       rx = "28",       fill = "#0f172a"     ),     tags$text(       x = "28",       y = "36",       fill = "#f8fafc",       `font-size` = "20",       `font-family` = "inherit",       `font-weight` = "700",       title     ),     tags$line(       x1 = pad_left,       y1 = pad_top + plot_h,       x2 = width - pad_right,       y2 = pad_top + plot_h,       stroke = "#334155",       `stroke-width` = "2"     ),     bars   ) } launch_scores <- c(   alpha = 42,   beta = 68,   gamma = 51,   delta = 91,   omega = 74 )  chart_a <- BarChart(launch_scores)  cat(   render(chart_a) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: now reuse chart base R dataset. VADeaths matrix mortality rates per 1000. ’ll take "Rural Male" column. chart need know data came . needed named numeric vector. sweet spot components: accept simple input, hide repetitive markup, return hypertext.tag.
code: rural_male <- VADeaths[, "Rural Male"]  chart_b <- BarChart(   values = rural_male,   width = 620,   height = 280,   title = "VADeaths: rural male" )  cat(   render(chart_b) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: svg can animate . javascript needed simple effects. animation tags children element animate.
code: #' An Animated Signal Dot #' #' @param x Number /// Required. #'          Horizontal centre. #' #' @param y Number /// Required. #'          Vertical centre. #' #' @param color String /// Optional. #'              Signal colour. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export SignalDot <- function(   x,   y,   color = "#22d3ee" ) {   tag_list(     tags$circle(       cx = x,       cy = y,       r = "10",       fill = color,       opacity = "0.55",       tags$animate(         attributeName = "r",         values = "10;34",         dur = "1.4s",         repeatCount = "indefinite"       ),       tags$animate(         attributeName = "opacity",         values = "0.55;0",         dur = "1.4s",         repeatCount = "indefinite"       )     ),     tags$circle(       cx = x,       cy = y,       r = "7",       fill = color     )   ) } live_signal <- Svg(   width = 520,   height = 180,   tags$rect(     x = "0",     y = "0",     width = "520",     height = "180",     rx = "28",     fill = "#020617"   ),   tags$text(     x = "42",     y = "76",     fill = "#f8fafc",     `font-size` = "28",     `font-family` = "inherit",     `font-weight` = "700",     "live signal"   ),   tags$text(     x = "42",     y = "108",     fill = "#94a3b8",     `font-size` = "15",     `font-family` = "inherit",     "animated with svg tags, rendered from r"   ),   SignalDot(x = 440, y = 90) )  cat(   render(live_signal) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: let’s end putting pieces together. next component returns small metric card: gradient panel, data-driven sparkline, live status dot. important part charting library. . important part hypertext gives raw building blocks. need graphic little custom plotting package, can build tags still keep code composable.
code: #' A Metric Card #' #' @param label String /// Required. #'              Metric label. #' #' @param value String /// Required. #'              Main value to display. #' #' @param points Numeric vector /// Required. #'               Sparkline points. #' #' @return `hypertext.tag` #' #' @export MetricCard <- function(label, value, points) {   width <- 560   height <- 220   x_values <- seq(56, 504, length.out = length(points))   point_span <- diff(range(points))   y_values <- if (point_span == 0) {     rep(115, length(points))   } else {     154 - (points - min(points)) / point_span * 78   }    path_d <- paste(     paste0(       c(         "M",         rep(x = "L", times = length(points) - 1)       ),       round(x_values, 1),       " ",       round(y_values, 1)     ),     collapse = " "   )    Svg(     width = width,     height = height,     tags$defs(       LinearGradient(         id = "metric-bg",         colors = c("#0f172a", "#312e81", "#701a75")       ),       LinearGradient(         id = "metric-line",         colors = c("#22d3ee", "#f0abfc")       )     ),     tags$rect(       x = "0",       y = "0",       width = width,       height = height,       rx = "30",       fill = "url(#metric-bg)"     ),     tags$text(       x = "36",       y = "50",       fill = "#cbd5e1",       `font-size` = "14",       `font-family` = "inherit",       `letter-spacing` = "1.6",       label     ),     tags$text(       x = "36",       y = "94",       fill = "#ffffff",       `font-size` = "42",       `font-family` = "inherit",       `font-weight` = "800",       value     ),     tags$path(       d = path_d,       fill = "none",       stroke = "url(#metric-line)",       `stroke-width` = "8",       `stroke-linecap` = "round",       `stroke-linejoin` = "round"     ),     lapply(       X = seq_along(points),       FUN = function(i) {         Orb(           cx = round(x_values[[i]], 1),           cy = round(y_values[[i]], 1),           r = 4,           fill = "#f8fafc",           opacity = 0.95         )       }     ),     SignalDot(x = 500, y = 50, color = "#f0abfc")   ) } metric <- MetricCard(   label = "RENDER PIPELINE",   value = "98.7%",   points = c(28, 35, 31, 48, 44, 62, 58, 79, 86) )  cat(   render(metric) )

title: svg with hypertext
text: svg hypertext follows rules html hypertext: named arguments become attributes. unnamed arguments become children. components functions return hypertext.tag objects. ... lets callers provide unknown children. lapply() can turn data repeated svg nodes. animation, gradients, paths just tags. know build components, already know shape svg workflow. new thing coordinate system.

text: Kennedy Mwavu. Author, maintainer. Sigflux. Copyright holder, funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mwavu K (2026). hypertext: 'HTML' Element Construction. R package version 1.2.0, https://sigflux.github.io/hypertext/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {hypertext: 'HTML' Element Construction},   author = {Kennedy Mwavu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.2.0},   url = {https://sigflux.github.io/hypertext/}, }

title: HTML Element Construction
text: html element construction R. hypertext provides deterministic, framework-agnostic DSL building html nodes rendering string. implement templating, dependency management, widgets framework integrations. heavily inspired {htmltools}.

title: HTML Element Construction
text: install stable version CRAN: get development version github:
code: install.packages("hypertext") devtools::install_github("sigflux/hypertext")

title: HTML Element Construction
code: library(hypertext)  page <- tag_list(   doctype(),   tags$html(     tags$head(       tags$title("hypertext")     ),     tags$body(       tags$h1("Hello"),       tags$p(         class = c("lead", "mb-2"),         "Server-side HTML."       ),       tags$input(         type = "text",         placeholder = "enter your nickname"       ),       tags$button(         "Click"       )     )   ) )  render(page)

title: HTML Element Construction
text: render() takes tag tree returns single HTML string: can render() directly html file supplying file parameter:
code:
x <- tags$p( class = "lead", "hello" ) # `x` contains the tag tree class(x) #> [1] "hypertext.tag" # rendering produces an HTML string: render(x) #> [1] "

hello

" library(hypertext) page <- tag_list( doctype(), tags$html( tags$head( tags$title("hypertext") ), tags$body( tags$h1("Hello"), tags$p( class = c("lead", "mb-2"), "Server-side HTML." ), tags$input( type = "text", placeholder = "enter your nickname" ), tags$button( "Click" ) ) ) ) render(x = page, file = "index.html")

title: HTML Element Construction
text: tag_list() groups sibling nodes without wrapping parent element.
code:
library(hypertext) header <- tag_list( tags$h1("hello"), tags$p( class = "lead", "welcome aboard." ) ) render(header) #> [1] "

hello

welcome aboard.

"

title: HTML Element Construction
text: raw_html() marks string pre-rendered HTML render() outputs verbatim, without escaping. useful injecting inline scripts, styles, SVG markup, content already valid HTML. doctype() convenience wrapper around raw_html("<!DOCTYPE html>").
code:
library(hypertext) page <- tags$div( raw_html("") ) render(page) #> [1] "
"

title: HTML Element Construction
text: tag() creates elements tag name, including web components custom elements built-tags list. takes 3 arguments: tag_name: name html element. ...: attributes (named) & children (unnamed). tag_type: either “normal” (default) standard html elements, “void” self-closing elements. nest freely built-tags: self-closing elements, set tag_type = "void":
code: library(hypertext)  content <- tag(   tag_name = "calcite-action-bar",   layout = "horizontal" )  render(content) #> [1] "<calcite-action-bar layout=\"horizontal\"></calcite-action-bar>" page <- tags$div(   class = "app",   tag(     tag_name = "calcite-shell",     tag(       tag_name = "calcite-shell-panel",       slot = "panel-start",       tag(         tag_name = "calcite-action-bar",         tag(           tag_name = "calcite-action",           text = "Layers",           icon = "layers"         ),         tag(           tag_name = "calcite-action",           text = "Basemaps",           icon = "basemap"         )       )     ),     tags$div(id = "map")   ) )  render(page) content <- tag(   tag_name = "my-icon",   name = "home",   tag_type = "void" )  render(content) #> [1] "<my-icon name=\"home\" />"

title: HTML Element Construction
text: ambiorix perfect example web framework find {hypertext} useful: shiny already {htmltools} tags internally, need {hypertext} shiny apps, case :
code: library(ambiorix) library(hypertext)  app <- Ambiorix$new(port = 3000L)  app$get("/", function(req, res) {   html <- tags$h1("hello, world!") |>     render()    res$send(html) })  app$get("/about", function(req, res) {   html <- tag_list(     tags$h1("about us"),     tags$p(       "minimal ",       tags$strong("html construction"),       " for R."     )   ) |>     render()    res$send(html) })  app$get("/team", function(req, res) {   teammates <- c("you", "me", "other")    html <- tags$div(     class = "team",     tags$p("meet the team:"),     tags$ul(       lapply(teammates, tags$li)     )   ) |>     render()    res$send(html) })  app$start() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(hypertext)  # use `hypertext::tags` explicitly to avoid clashing with `shiny::tags`. ht <- hypertext::tags  card <- function(title, body) {   ht$div(     class = "card mt-3",     ht$div(       class = "card-header",       title     ),     ht$div(       class = "card-body",       ht$p(         class = "card-text",         body       )     )   ) }  content <- ht$div(   class = "container py-4",   card("First card", "Some quick example text."),   card("Second card", "Another body of text.") ) |>   render()  ui <- page(   theme = bs_theme(version = 5L),   # hypertext renders an HTML string, so wrap in shiny::HTML()   HTML(content) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Render the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration — doctype
text: Convenience wrapper around raw_html() returns HTML5 document-type declaration. Useful building full page.

title: Render the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration — doctype
code: doctype()

title: Render the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration — doctype
text: "hypertext.raw" object containing <!DOCTYPE html>.

title: Render the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration — doctype
code:
page <- tag_list( doctype(), tags$html( tags$head(tags$title("Home")), tags$body(tags$h1("Welcome")) ) ) render(page) #> [1] "Home

Welcome

"

what: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
text: Wraps one character strings render() outputs verbatim, without escaping HTML special characters.

title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
code: raw_html(...)

title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
text: ... Character strings raw HTML /// Optional.

title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
text: character vector class "hypertext.raw".

title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
text: useful injecting pre-rendered HTML, inline <script>/<style> blocks, SVG markup, content entity-escaped.

title: Mark a string as raw HTML — raw_html
code:
raw_html("") #> render(tags$div(raw_html("already formatted"))) #> [1] "
already formatted
"

what: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
text: Converts hypertext.tag object (descendants) HTML string. Text children escaped; nested hypertext.tag children rendered recursively.

title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
code: render(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'hypertext.tag' render(x, file = "", write_mode = c("overwrite", "append"), ...)  # Default S3 method render(x, file = "", write_mode = c("overwrite", "append"), ...)  # S3 method for class 'hypertext.raw' render(x, file = "", write_mode = c("overwrite", "append"), ...)  # S3 method for class 'list' render(x, file = "", write_mode = c("overwrite", "append"), ...)

title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
text: x hypertext.tag object, string, list /// Required. ... arguments passed methods. file String /// Optional. Path file print . write_mode String /// Optional. Either "overwrite" (default) overwrites contents file, "append" appends HTML string file.

title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
text: single character string HTML.

title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
text: file provided, rendered HTML written specified file via cat() HTML string returned invisibly.

title: Render an HTML node tree to a character string — render
code:
page <- tags$html( tags$head( tags$title("Home") ), tags$body( tags$h1("Welcome") ) ) # return HTML as a string: render(page) #> [1] "Home

Welcome

" if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # write to a file: render(page, file = "index.html") } # }

what: Create an HTML element node — tag
title: Create an HTML element node — tag
text: Low-level constructor. Named arguments become attributes; unnamed arguments become children (text nested nodes).

title: Create an HTML element node — tag
code: tag(tag_name, ..., tag_type = c("normal", "void"))

title: Create an HTML element node — tag
text: tag_name String /// Required. HTML element name. ... Attributes (named) children (unnamed) /// Optional. tag_type String /// Optional. Either "normal" (default) standard element children, "void" self-closing element whose children ignored.

title: Create an HTML element node — tag
text: List class "hypertext.tag" components tag, attrs, children, tag_type.

title: Create an HTML element node — tag
code: # web component tag(tag_name = "calcite-action-bar", layout = "horizontal") #> <calcite-action-bar layout="horizontal"></calcite-action-bar>   # custom element with children tag(   tag_name = "my-card",   class = "shadow",   tag(tag_name = "my-card-header", "Title"),   tag(tag_name = "my-card-body", "Content") ) #> <my-card class="shadow"><my-card-header>Title</my-card-header><my-card-body>Content</my-card-body></my-card>   # custom void element tag(tag_name = "my-icon", name = "home", tag_type = "void") #> <my-icon name="home" />

what: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
title: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
text: Useful want collect sibling nodes single object without wrapping parent element.

title: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
code: tag_list(...)

title: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
text: ... Tags, text, renderable objects /// Optional.

title: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
text: list class "hypertext.tag.list".

title: Create a tag list (sibling container) — tag_list
code:
tl <- tag_list(tags$p("one"), tags$p("two")) render(tl) #> [1] "

one

two

"

what: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
text: named list functions, one per HTML SVG element. Access individual tags tags$div(...), tags$p(...), tags$svg(...), etc.

title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
code: tags

title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
text: Named arguments become HTML attributes; unnamed arguments become child nodes text content.

title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
text: Pass NA TRUE attribute value produce valueless attribute (e.g. disabled). FALSE NULL suppresses attribute entirely.

title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
text: Character vectors length > 1 collapsed space, class = c("", "b") renders class="b".

title: HTML and SVG tag functions — tags
code:
tags$p(class = "lead", "Hello, world!") #>

Hello, world!

render(tags$div(id = "app", tags$h1("Title"))) #> [1] "

Title

"
